message:
chore(beads): update issue status notes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1437,7 +1437,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-db300.7","title":"Track G: correctness, fault injection, and final scorecard","description":"## Goal
This track makes sure the optimization program stays scientifically honest. Aggressive concurrency and commit-path changes can create correctness, crash-recovery, and fairness regressions that only show up under stress. The project needs a final gate that says exactly what must be true before claiming meaningful progress over SQLite.

## Acceptance Criteria
- There are explicit rollout criteria rather than vague optimism.
- New commit-path machinery has corresponding correctness and crash-validation coverage.
- Final results can be explained causally, not just observed numerically.
- This bead set explicitly includes comprehensive unit-test, end-to-end, and structured-logging obligations rather than treating verification as implied follow-up work.","design":"- Keep fault injection, crash simulation, and end-to-end fairness in the loop.
- Define explicit throughput, retry, and latency scorecard thresholds.
- Require a final decision record that explains what moved wall time/cycles and what failed to matter.","acceptance_criteria":"- There are explicit rollout criteria rather than vague optimism.
- New commit-path machinery has corresponding correctness and crash-validation coverage.
- Final results can be explained causally, not just observed numerically.
- This bead set explicitly includes comprehensive unit-test, end-to-end, and structured-logging obligations rather than treating verification as implied follow-up work.","notes":"If this track is weak, the rest of the epic becomes a pile of anecdotal benchmark wins. The point is not to slow optimization down; the point is to make each win believable and durable.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-10T04:47:25.833279483Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:55:31.846710667Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-g","evidence","quality-gate","testing","track-g","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2262,"issue_id":"bd-db300.7","author":"codex","text":"Track G flow: G5 per-bead verification matrix, G6 structured logging contract, G1 comparison-harness alignment, G7 one-command verification suites, G2 crash/fault coverage, G3 exit criteria, G4 final scorecard. This track exists to prevent fake wins and missing tests from slipping through under performance pressure.","created_at":"2026-03-10T06:24:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.7.1","title":"G1: align end-to-end comparison reporting across SQLite, MVCC, and single-writer","description":"## Goal
Extend the end-to-end comparison harness so SQLite, MVCC, and forced single-writer runs are reported with identical retry semantics, comparable counters, and the canonical artifact layout. This bead ensures the final scorecard is not undermined by subtle harness asymmetry.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The comparison harness reports SQLite, MVCC, and single-writer results under the same retry/accounting policy.
- The output is aligned with the canonical matrix and artifact layout from Track A.
- A representative matrix run proves the reporting is usable for final comparison work.
- The scripts, harness changes, or reports in this bead emit structured logs and artifact manifests rich enough to reproduce results without reconstructing the workflow by hand.","acceptance_criteria":"- The comparison harness reports SQLite, MVCC, and single-writer results under the same retry/accounting policy.
- The output is aligned with the canonical matrix and artifact layout from Track A.
- A representative matrix run proves the reporting is usable for final comparison work.
- The scripts, harness changes, or reports in this bead emit structured logs and artifact manifests rich enough to reproduce results without reconstructing the workflow by hand.","notes":"Planning note:
The G1 container is narrowed to the actual measurement/reporting leaves: A3.2 for topology-aware counters, A3.3 for artifact/provenance shape, and A4.1 for metric naming. That keeps the comparison-reporting frontier tied to the real prerequisites instead of the broad Track A container.

User-facing intent:
- the comparison harness should be trustworthy enough that users can read one row across SQLite, MVCC, and forced single-writer and know it represents the same work under the same accounting contract","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:10:00.556506195Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-14T23:52:42.143216222Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-g","e2e","evidence","harness","track-g","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:49:43.906158400Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:49:53.748218014Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:50:03.622468417Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.7.1.1","title":"G1.1: align retry and accounting semantics across comparison modes","description":"## Goal
align retry and accounting semantics across comparison modes

## Checklist
1. Make retry semantics match across SQLite, MVCC, and forced single-writer.
2. Align accounting rules so the modes report comparable work.
3. Keep the result ready for representative matrix runs.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Make retry semantics match across SQLite, MVCC, and forced single-writer.
2. Align accounting rules so the modes report comparable work.
3. Keep the result ready for representative matrix runs.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Make retry semantics match across SQLite, MVCC, and forced single-writer.
2. Align accounting rules so the modes report comparable work.
3. Keep the result ready for representative matrix runs.","notes":"Comparison-semantic obligations:
- define how retries, aborts, busy handling, setup failures, and partial-run accounting are represented in SQLite, MVCC, and forced single-writer results so cross-mode rows are truly comparable
- make explicit how SQLite rows express zero-retry semantics without hiding meaningful stalls or error cases
- require representative proof that the same matrix row means the same unit of work across all three modes","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:51:27.051940916Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-16T04:58:37.874166823Z","closed_at":"2026-03-16T04:58:37.873610280Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-g","e2e","harness","track-g"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:53:25.788507943Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:53:35.794085901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:46:56.951896925Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-db300.7.1.2","title":"G1.2: align counter schema and canonical artifact layout across modes","description":"## Goal
align counter schema and canonical artifact layout across modes

## Checklist
1. Make counters and reported fields align across all three modes.
2. Emit the canonical artifact layout from the comparison harness.
3. Keep the result reusable by G7 and G4.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Make counters and reported fields align across all three modes.
2. Emit the canonical artifact layout from the comparison harness.
3. Keep the result reusable by G7 and G4.","acceptance_criteria":"1. SQLite, MVCC, and forced single-writer runs emit an aligned counter schema with stable field names, units, and semantics for cross-mode-comparable metrics, while clearly separating mode-specific counters instead of silently overloading them.
2. The comparison harness emits the canonical artifact layout and row identity fields needed for downstream suite packaging and final scorecard work.
3. At least one representative cross-mode row can be inspected mechanically without custom translation, proving that counters, manifests, and artifact paths line up as intended.
4. The result is directly reusable by G7 suite entrypoints and final scorecard reporting.","notes":"Artifact-layout obligations:
- align counter names, field meanings, units, and artifact directory or manifest layout across SQLite, MVCC, and forced single-writer runs
- require row identity to include fixture, workload, concurrency, mode, placement profile, build profile, source revision, and run identifier so downstream reporting never has to infer them
- define how mode-specific fields are surfaced without contaminating the cross-mode comparable set
- optimize for users by making side-by-side row comparison readable and trustworthy without hand-normalizing artifacts","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:51:41.835597727Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-14T23:59:43.323328396Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["artifacts","benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-g","logging","track-g"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:46:35.735262200Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:46:46.288116176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-db300.7.1.3","title":"G1.3: prove representative canonical matrix comparison run","description":"## Goal
prove representative canonical matrix comparison run

## Checklist
1. Run a representative canonical comparison after semantic alignment.
2. Confirm the report is usable for final comparison work.
3. Package representative artifacts for downstream reporting.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Run a representative canonical comparison after semantic alignment.
2. Confirm the report is usable for final comparison work.
3. Package representative artifacts for downstream reporting.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The representative proof run spans all three comparison modes and covers enough workload geometry to validate the reporting contract, not just a single flattering row.
2. The run produces aligned manifests, counters, structured logs, and artifact paths that can be inspected mechanically using the contracts from G1.1 and G1.2.
3. The closure bundle includes a short interpretation note explaining whether any remaining comparison asymmetry or artifact-layout confusion was discovered.
4. The proof run is small enough to be rerun routinely but rich enough that future agents can trust the comparison harness before launching wider matrices.","notes":"Representative-run obligations:
- choose a compact but honest subset of canonical rows that exercises all three modes and more than one workload geometry
- require the resulting bundle to demonstrate aligned retry/accounting semantics, aligned artifact layout, and clean rerun metadata
- preserve a short interpretation note describing what the proof run validated and what still remains for the full scorecard
- optimize for users by giving them one trustworthy sanity-check run they can invoke before spending time or machine budget on the full matrix","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:51:56.740099374Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-14T23:59:50.769882133Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-g","evidence","realdb","track-g"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.7.2","title":"G2: add crash and fault-injection coverage for batched WAL and group commit","description":"## Goal
Add crash and fault-injection coverage specifically around the new batched WAL path and any future group-commit or durability-lane work. This bead exists because commit-path optimizations are exactly where silent correctness regressions become catastrophic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Batched WAL append and publish changes have explicit crash/fault coverage.
- Recovery behavior is checked against the expected invariants.
- Any future group-commit prototype is brought under the same fault model before being taken seriously.
- Targeted unit tests are added or refreshed for the exact invariants and regressions touched by this bead.
- Where behavior crosses subsystem boundaries, canonical-matrix end-to-end validation with detailed structured logging is added or updated alongside the change.","acceptance_criteria":"- Batched WAL append and publish changes have explicit crash/fault coverage.
- Recovery behavior is checked against the expected invariants.
- Any future group-commit prototype is brought under the same fault model before being taken seriously.
- Targeted unit tests are added or refreshed for the exact invariants and regressions touched by this bead.
- Where behavior crosses subsystem boundaries, canonical-matrix end-to-end validation with detailed structured logging is added or updated alongside the change.","notes":"This bead should focus on the new code paths created by C1/C2/C5 rather than generic crash testing that already exists elsewhere in the project.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:10:57.193516338Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:55:32.506398588Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crash","db300-program","db300-track-g","durability","fault-injection","testing","track-g","verification","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

## Checklist
1. Enumerate crash and fault cases specific to C1, C2, and future C5 work.
2. Tie each case to the new code paths rather than generic crash testing.
3. Keep the matrix ready for targeted injection hooks.
